title: Gilramos Libkath
content:

{{Quote|It stinks of treachery. It stinks of Gilramos Libkath.|Jabba the Hutt|<ref name="Hunt" />}}
'''Gilramos Libkath''' was a conniving [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]], with an unusually deep voice and wearing a large miter, who served as [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] [[Aide/Legends|aide]] and [[Contract-Scribe]]. Libkath was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] on Tatooine, a [[Star/Legends|sun]]-scorched world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During his time on that arid planet, the Neimoidian built up a small criminal business, using a group of kidnapped [[Youngling/Legends|children]] in thefts. At that time, he was known to his young [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] as "the Master." Libkath's criminal activities were brought to an end in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] put a price on his head. Two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] named [[Durge/Legends|Durge]] and the ten-year-old [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], were responsible for Gilramos Libkath's [[Death/Legends|demise]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|He gives us shelter and food. Not much, but better than nothing. He protects us from the Hutt gangsters. In return we do what he asks.|Ygabba, on Libkath|<ref name="Hunt" />}}
[[File:Tatooine-TCW.png|thumb|left|150px|Tatooine, Libkath's planet of exile]]
Gilramos Libkath was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]],<ref name="Hunt" /> a [[Near-Duros]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="GMR2">{{GamerCite|2|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Duros}}</ref> that hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Neimoidia/Legends|Neimoidia]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies Regions]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> In the last decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Libkath came to be affiliated with the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a Neimoidian-dominated trade cartel and megacorporation, serving as an [[Aide/Legends|assistant]] and [[Contract-Scribe]] to its leader, the [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]].<ref name="SWC:P" /> For unspecified reasons, Libkath was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and,<ref name="Hunt" /> by [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],<ref name="27 BBY">In the novel ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'', [[Gab'borah Hise]] mentioned that his daughter [[Ygabba Hise|Ygabba]] was kidnapped by Libkath on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] "five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] ago." According to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', the events of that novel occurred in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Therefore, Libkath must have been present on Tatooine as early as 27 BBY.</ref> he lived on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Despite his eviction, the Contract-Scribe retained connections with the Federation.<ref name="Hunt" />

On Tatooine, Gilramos Libkath became a minor personality in the underworld of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], one of the planet's expansive [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]]. The Neimoidian would kidnap [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] and street urchins whom he called his "children." In exchange for a roof over their heads and protection against the gangsters, "Master Libkath" forced the younglings to steal for him. He kept them under control using [[Tracer orb|devices]] implanted in their hands that released [[Poison/Legends|toxins]] in the body if the children left the planet. Those who failed Libkath or refused to obey him were sold as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] or indentured servants. One of its little victims was [[Ygabba Hise|Ygabba]], the daughter of the Tatooinian [[Chef/Legends|chef]] [[Gab'borah Hise]].<ref name="Hunt" />

Libkath even went as far as to steal weapon and [[Spice/Legends|spice]] shipments from [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] of Tatooine, that he would sell to his old friends of the Trade Federation. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Federation had joined the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist Confederacy]],<ref name="Hunt" /> an alliance that advocated the secession of worlds from the Galactic Republic.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Such bold actions earned Libkath the Hutt's hatred.<ref name="Hunt" />

[[File:BobaFett-LotJExtendedBase.png|thumb|150px|Boba Fett, the boy who killed Libkath]]
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], intent on getting rid of that Neimoidian busybody, Jabba sicced two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] on Libkath: a young Human named [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] as well as the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] hunter [[Durge/Legends|Durge]]. "The Master" was well-protected, however, rarely appearing in person and instead communicating with his thieving children via [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] transmissions. He also owned a personal complement of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to take care of potential attackers.<ref name="Hunt" />

The bounty hunters, despite attempting to kill one another every step of the way, managed to discover the Neimoidian's hideout. They caught Libkath off guard and cornered him in the hull of a heavily damaged [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. Fett then smashed Libkath in the head twice with a brick before swooping in and stealing his miter. When Libkath tried to flee, Durge shot a crate of weapons, provoking an explosion that blew up the Neimoidian's lair. "The Master" and all of his attending battle droids perished in the blaze, while Fett successuflly escaped the burning hideout in the derelict Theed cruiser, taking the enslaved children with him. The Neimoidian's miter was given to Jabba as a proof that he was dead. Jabba then unceremoniously dropped the miter in a [[Fire/Legends|fire]], letting the flames destroy the last trace of Gilramos Libkath for good.<ref name="Hunt" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Failure means destruction. Do not fail.|Gilramos Libkath, to the children|<ref name="Hunt" />}}
[[File:BobaFett_4_art.jpg|thumb|left|220x220px|Boba Fett's Mission to Tatooine, which ended in Libkath's death.]]
Like most Neimoidians,<ref name="NEGAS" /> Libkath was tall and thin,<ref name="Hunt" /> a racial trait due to the heavier gravity on their home planet of [[Neimoidia/Legends|Neimoidia]].<ref name="NEGAS" /> He had a long, noseless head and, because of his species' iron-based sclera pigmentation,<ref name="TPMV">''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> he sported orange [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that reminded the young Boba Fett of Tatooine's twin suns.<ref name="Hunt" /> Compared to many of his kind, Libkath had sickly white skin. Those traits, along with his gnarled hands, made him frightening to his young slaves. In order to further enhance his towering height and scary composure, Gilramos Libkath wore a tall, [[Crown/Legends|crown]]-like black miter and a set of deep-blue shimmering [[Clothing/Legends|robes]].<ref name="Hunt" /> He shared many physical traits and tastes in fashion with three other important Neimoidians:<ref name="SWC:P" /> [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Daultay Dofine/Legends|Daultay Dofine]],<ref name="TPMV" /> [[Settlement Officer/Legends|Settlement Officer]] [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]],<ref name="AOC">{{Film|II}}</ref> and a [[Unidentified Neimoidian (Muunilinst)|representative]] of the Confederacy during the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]].<ref name="CWC4">{{CW|4}}</ref> Libkath had an unusually deep voice sounding like a low, yet smooth hiss. Like all Neimoidians, Gilramos Libkath was extremely vain. He placed huge value on his hat, which was a symbol of power and social prestige.<ref name="Hunt" />

==Skills and abilities==
For a Neimoidian, Libkath had a firm grip, strong enough to clutch and hold the young Boba Fett.<ref name="Hunt" /> Although Fett was only ten years old at the time, he had received basic [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] training in combat skills.<ref name="AOCV">''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The Neimoidian was also capable of firing bolts of red lightning with his hands. However, it is unknown whether it was a real [[Force power/Legends|manifestation]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] known as [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]—in which case Libkath would have been [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]—or just an elaborate trick,<ref name="Hunt" /> like [[Trioculus' lightning device|the one]] used by the self-proclaimed [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Trioculus]] about [[5 ABY/Legends|thirty years later]].<ref name="GDV">''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''</ref> While Fett did witness the Neimoidian throwing a salvo of crimson lightnings, he was unable to determine how he had achieved it.<ref name="Hunt" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|So publishing dubbed the non-Haako, non-Dofine Neimoidian "Gilramos Libkath," which was a two-for Tuckerism of two production folks, Kathryn Ramos and Gillian Libbert. The proper Haako mask would resurface in time for Episode III.|[[Pablo Hidalgo]]|<ref name="Twitter">{{Twitter|username=pabl0hidalgo|url=status/1497969309682843651|name=[[Pablo Hidalgo]]|date=2022-02-27|archivedate=20220227161826}}</ref>}}
[[File:FakeLibkath.png|thumb|150px|Nute Gunray and "Gilramos Libkath" in the ''Visual Dictionary'']]
The character of Gilramos Libkath was originally intended to be the Neimoidian seen with Nute Gunray on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] and in ''[[CIS Shadowfeed Dispatch 14:10:05 Edition]]'' in [[Star Wars Insider 71|''Star Wars Insider'' 71]]. He was named after costume supervisor [[Gillian Libbert]] and production controller [[Kathryn Ramos]].<ref name="SWC:P" /> Libkath was also identified by name in ''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary]]''.<ref name="AOCV" />

However, the published version of the ''Attack of the Clones'' script attributed Libkath's dialogue lines to Rune Haako, a character who had appeared alongside Gunray in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']],<ref name="Holoc">{{Blog|holocron|29|Mistaken Identities}}</ref> whereas the movie credits identified him as [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Lott Dod/Legends|Lott Dod]], another Neimoidian from the previous movie.<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> Still, the Neimoidian shown in ''Attack of the Clones'' neither resembled or sounded like Haako or even Dod.<ref name="TPM" /> The official explanation by [[Lucasfilm]] was that Rune Haako's original mask had been lost between the two movies and his character was attributed Daultay Dofine's mask instead.<ref name="Holoc" />

At the end of the day, despite his previous identification as Gilramos Libkath, it has since been firmly established that the Neimoidian with Gunray at Geonosis was indeed Rune Haako.<ref name="Holoc" /> With the release of ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]'', the mislabeled picture showing the two Neimoidians together was altered to reflect the change.<ref name="cvd">''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> Nonetheless, that [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] did not stop Gilramos Libkath from being established as a different character in ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'', the fourth book in a series of young reader novels that chronicled the adventures of a young Boba Fett.<ref name="Hunt" />

==Appearances==
